title: Search query and focused issue are encoded in the URL so any state can be reached via deep link
description:
Given a user is viewing the console board with a search query active

When I copy the URL from the browser address bar
And open it in a new tab

Then the new tab renders the same search results

Given an issue is focused (showing issue and descendants)

When I copy the URL from the browser address bar
And open it in a new tab

Then the new tab shows the same focused issue and its descendants

Acceptance criteria:
- Search query is encoded as a query parameter: ?search=<query>
- Focused issue is encoded in the URL path (already partially works via /issues/:parentId/all pattern)
- View mode is in the URL path (already works)
- On page load, the frontend reads ALL state from the URL and renders accordingly
- Refreshing the page preserves the complete UI state
- This means SSE ui_control events just trigger URL changes, and the frontend renders from the URL
creator: ryan.porter

comments.author: ryan.porter
comments.text: Implemented: search query and focused issue are now encoded in the URL as query parameters (?search=, ?focused=). parseRoute() reads them on every parse. buildUrl() helper constructs URLs with these params. Handlers for search change/clear, focus, clear_focus, and set_search all update the URL via replaceState. Back/forward navigation hydrates React state from URL params. Build and tsc both clean.
